nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Rye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone living at a place where rye (Old English ryge) was grown, or perhaps an occupational name for someone who grew or sold it.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads so named, most of them from Old Norse rjóthr ‘clearing in a forest’.

variants.name: Ryer
meanings: English (Sussex and Kent): probably from Rye (see Rea ) + the suffix -er, and is thus synonymous with Middle English atte Rye, denoting someone who lived by a river or an island. This type of formation is common in Sussex and Kent.
Americanized form of German   Reier   ‘heron’ or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.
Americanized form of Slovenian Rajar or Rajer (see Rayer ).

variants.name: Romney
meanings: English: habitational name from Romney (Kent), from an uncertain first element (perhaps an Old English personal name Rūmen) + Old English ēa ‘river, stream’. Compare Rye 2.

variants.name: Rea
meanings: English: from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atter ye ‘at the island’ (Old English ēg, īeg). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on an island or patch of firm ground surrounded by marsh, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Rye (Sussex), The Rye in High Wycombe (Buckinghamshire), Rye Hill in Epping Upland (Essex), Rye Mill in Feering (Essex), and Rye House in Stanstead Abbots (Hertfordshire). Alternatively, the name may also arise from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atter eye ‘at the river’ (Old English ēa) and may also be topographic, for someone who lived near a river or stream, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Rea in Hempsted (Gloucestershire), Ray Lodge in Lingfield (Surrey), and Rye Farm in Cranleigh (Surrey). Due to the similarity of Middle English forms it is now impossible to distinguish between the two explanations. See also Nye .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Rye was born about 1155, in Lincolnshire, England, his father, Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham, was 9 and his mother, Margaret William Wales, was 8. He had at least 6 sons with Mrs Robert de Rye.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Rye est né environ 1155, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham, avait 9 ans et sa mère, Margaret William Wales, avait 8 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils avec Mrs Robert de Rye.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Rye um 1155 in Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham 9 und seine Mutter Margaret William Wales 8. Er hatte mit Mrs Robert de Rye mindestens 6 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Rye è nato il circa 1155 nel Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham, aveva 9 anni e il suo madre, Margaret William Wales, aveva 8 anni. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli con Mrs Robert de Rye.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Rye(이)가 대략 1155년 영국 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham(은)는 9세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret William Wales(은)는 8세였다. 그는 Mrs Robert de Rye(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Rye nasceu aproximadamente 1155, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham, tinha 9 anos e sua mãe, Margaret William Wales, tinha 8 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos com Mrs Robert de Rye.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Rye родился около 1155 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham было 9 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret William Wales, 8. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей от Mrs Robert de Rye.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Rye nació aproximadamente 1155, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Hubert III de Rie - Baron of Higham, tenía 9 años y su mamá, Margaret William Wales, tenía 8 años. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos con Mrs Robert de Rye.
